frame_analyses.description: The suture is being tied into the first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: The instruments are positioned to begin the surgeon's knot.

frame_analyses.description: The suture is being positioned through the drain marks.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement appears close to the marks with slight deviation.

frame_analyses.description: The first throw, a surgeon's knot, is being tightened.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper tension is applied to secure the first throw.

frame_analyses.description: The second throw is being tied with a 'C' loop.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: The hand is switched and the loop is correctly formed.

frame_analyses.description: The third throw is being completed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand is switched again, maintaining proper technique.

frame_analyses.description: The third throw is being tightened.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: The throw appears secure with no visible slipping.

frame_analyses.description: The suture ends are being cut to complete the task.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both ends are cut close to the knot.

knot_assessments.notes: The surgeon's knot is tied securely.
Proper hand switch and loop formation.
Final throw is secure with appropriate switch.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: The slit in the drain is completely closed with no gaps visible.

technique_summary: The participant demonstrated good technique with secure knot tying and minimal suture deviation. The task was completed within a reasonable time frame.
improvement_suggestions: Focus on reducing deviation from the suture marks for better precision.
Ensure consistent hand switching to maintain throwing technique.
strengths: Secure knot tying with proper tension.
